codeQuality.notes: Well-structured e2e test suite with clear documentation, descriptive test names, and proper JSDoc headers for each file. Tests are logically grouped by concern (full journey, voice, offline). Playwright config is sensible with sequential execution to avoid database conflicts, appropriate timeouts (90s), and failure-only screenshots/videos. All 246 unit tests continue to pass, TypeScript compiles cleanly, and ESLint reports no errors.

testCoverage.notes: 12 e2e tests across 3 files comprehensively cover the three verification steps: (1) full-journey.spec.ts tests profile creation → meditation session → message sending → session end → navigation + page reload persistence (3 tests). (2) voice-session.spec.ts validates voice controls presence, TTS toggle, voice output bridge, and keyboard accessibility (4 tests). (3) offline-mode.spec.ts covers profile display, history, settings, profile settings, and session-in-history (5 tests). Gap: actual voice transcription/integration tests are absent — only UI element presence and toggling are verified. This is expected given Playwright's microphone limitations, but means the STT/TTS server integration itself is not covered by e2e tests.

integrationQuality.notes: Tests correctly integrate with the app's API for data clearing (fetch to localhost:3100/api/export/clear), use proper role-based selectors matching the ARIA-labeled app, and verify cross-area flows (profile → meditation → history → settings). Playwright config uses the correct baseURL (localhost:3101) which matches the Vite dev server that proxies /api to the backend. Tests correctly rely on localStorage-based session restoration tested in the session persistence test.

issues.description: Inconsistent clearAllData helper error handling: full-journey.spec.ts (line 14-17) throws on fetch failure, but voice-session.spec.ts and offline-mode.spec.ts silently ignore errors. This could mask test setup failures in 9 of 12 tests. Recommend unifying to throw consistently.

Hardcoded waitForTimeout sleeps: full-journey.spec.ts uses waitForTimeout(8000) (line 91) and offline-mode.spec.ts uses waitForTimeout(1000) (line 124). These are brittle — if Ollama is slow or fast, the timing may be wrong. Prefer waitFor assertions on specific UI states or polling patterns.

Repetitive profile creation boilerplate: ~10 lines of profile setup are copy-pasted in every test. A reusable `createProfile(page)` helper with the conditional onboarding check would reduce maintenance burden and make tests more readable.

Playwright config lacks webServer configuration — services (backend on :3100, frontend on :3101) must be started manually before running tests. The worker attempted to add webServer config per the transcript but it is not in the final commit. Including webServer would make the test suite self-contained and reproducible on any machine.

Settings 'Clear All Data' button is not accessible via getByRole('button') selector — no aria-label on the button. The offline-mode test works around this by selecting the danger section container instead. Adding aria-label="Clear All Data" in Settings.tsx would improve accessibility.
Playwright config only tests Chromium. Adding Firefox and WebKit projects would catch cross-browser regressions, especially for Web Audio API (voice) and WebRTC (microphone) features.

Voice tests verify UI presence and toggle state but not actual transcription or speech synthesis. The STT/TTS server integration layer should ideally be tested at the API level (backend integration tests) since e2e testing of microphone/speaker is not practical with Playwright.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Common UI selector patterns are not documented. The worker spent time figuring out that the onboarding submit button says 'Begin Journey' (not 'Begin'), the name input label is 'Name', and navigation links use specific ARIA labels. These patterns are scattered across components.
sharedStateObservations.description: Adding a 'Common Selectors' section to the frontend-worker SKILL.md or AGENTS.md documenting key ARIA labels, button text, and input labels would save future workers from trial-and-error. Examples: 'Begin Journey' button, 'Begin Meditation' button, 'Begin meditation session' button, textbox label 'Type your message to Marcus', link labels 'Meditation', 'History', 'Profile', 'Settings', 'Home'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Playwright config has no webServer block despite the transcript showing the worker attempted to add one. This means e2e tests require manual service startup.
sharedStateObservations.description: services.yaml documents all services but the test runner config doesn't leverage them. Adding Playwright webServer configuration (or documenting the manual startup requirement clearly) would improve reproducibility.

summary: The 'polish-cross-flow-complete' feature delivers a robust e2e test suite with 12 passing Playwright tests across three well-organized files. All three verification areas from the feature description are covered: (1) full user journey from profile creation through meditation to history, (2) voice-enhanced session (UI controls, TTS toggling), and (3) offline/read-only mode resilience. The implementation validates all six cross-area flows listed in fulfills (VAL-CROSS-001 through VAL-CROSS-003). Test quality is good with clear JSDoc structure, semantic selector usage, and proper beforeEach data clearing. No blocking issues found. Non-blocking improvements include: unifying error handling in helpers, replacing hardcoded sleeps with proper waits, extracting profile creation into a shared helper, and adding webServer config for self-contained test execution.
